title: Users stopped receiving 'mention' email notifications starting Tuesday
problem_reported: Starting Tuesday morning, customers say they are no longer getting email notifications when a teammate @mentions them. In-app notifications still appear. The in-app notification count matches what we would expect - only the emails are missing.
expected_behavior: On a mention, we enqueue a MentionNotification job; the worker renders the template and hands it to SendGrid for delivery.
actual_behavior: In-app notification is created. No corresponding SendGrid API call is recorded. No delivery, no bounce, no suppression - the message was never sent.
diagnosis_steps: Confirmed the in-app notification row exists for a reproduction mention.
Looked for the corresponding MentionNotification job in the queue - it was enqueued and marked complete, duration 2 ms.
Two-millisecond job completion is the same shape as the Stripe bug last month - suspect an early return.
Checked recent deploys: Monday afternoon we shipped a refactor that added a 'user has email notifications enabled' preference check.
Queried the preferences table: the default for the new column was NULL, and the check treats NULL as 'disabled'. Every user who had never explicitly toggled the preference is silently opted out.
resolution: Shipped a migration that set the new preference column to TRUE for all existing users (preserving prior behavior) and set the column default to TRUE going forward. Confirmed the check treats TRUE as 'send.'
confirmation: Triggered a test mention for five accounts that had been affected. All five received the email within a minute. Checked SendGrid dashboard for a return to normal volume - mention emails back to baseline within an hour. Added a test that fails if a new notification preference is added without a safe default.
